_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/Lower GI bleeding/ : Lower GI bleeding_Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + Lower GI bleeding_PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Diagnosis of Lower GI hemorrhage backed by clinical manifestation of bleeding emanating from a source distal to the ligament of Treitz.

Common symptoms include: Bright red blood from the anus (streaks of blood or larger clots), Melena (black sticky stool) with foul smell, Belly pain, Light headedness, Fainting, Chest discomfort, Nausea, Difficulty breathing.

preauth.label: Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment

claim.label: Detailed Indoor Case Papers and Treatment details

ppd_preauth.q: Were the Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, and planned line of treatment submitted?
Was the CBC, Platelets reports submitted?
Was the Fecal tagging / Colonoscopy submitted?
Was the patient had complaints of presence of blood in stool?

cpd_claim.q: Was the Detailed ICPs with all the Treatment details submitted?
Was the detailed discharge summary submitted?

common_queries: Patient's clinical notes do not document complaints of blood in stool.
Clinical Notes including evaluation findings, indications for the procedure, or planned line of treatment are missing or incomplete at pre-authorization.
CBC or Platelets reports are missing at pre-authorization.
Fecal tagging or Colonoscopy reports are missing at pre-authorization.
Detailed Indoor Case Papers or Treatment details are missing or incomplete at claim submission.

additional_information.min_doctor_qualification_desirable: MD/DNB Equivalent (in General Medicine / Pediatric Medicine)
clinical_key_pointers: Proceed with Lower GI hemorrhage only if diagnosis made is backed by clinical manifestation: LGIB historically has been defined as bleeding that emanates from a source distal to the ligament of Treitz.
